exercises.title: Size Calculation Verification
exercises.description: Calculate and verify ML-DSA key and signature sizes.
tasks: Calculate public key size using: pk_size = 32 + k × 320
Calculate signature size for each variant
Verify your calculations match the FIPS 204 specification
Calculate total size for a 3-certificate chain for each variant
Compare with RSA-3072 and ECDSA P-384 certificate chains
exercises.expectedOutput: Table of calculated sizes with verification against spec
hints: A certificate includes public key + signature

exercises.title: Parameter Selection Case Studies
exercises.description: Recommend ML-DSA variants for specific deployment scenarios.
tasks: Case 1: E-commerce website TLS certificate (renewed annually)
Case 2: National identity card digital signatures (valid 10 years)
Case 3: Embedded IoT sensor firmware updates (constrained device)
Case 4: Government classified document signing system
Provide detailed justification for each recommendation
exercises.expectedOutput: Case study document with justified parameter recommendations
hints: Consider: signature lifetime, bandwidth, device constraints, policy requirements
IoT may need to consider both signing device and verification device

exercises.title: Security Margin Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze security margins for each ML-DSA variant.
tasks: Research Core-SVP estimates for each ML-DSA parameter set
Calculate security margin above the NIST category target
Analyze what happens if lattice attacks improve by 20%
Compare security margins with ML-KEM variants
Recommend minimum acceptable variant for 50-year document validity
exercises.expectedOutput: Security analysis document with margin calculations

exercises.title: Performance Benchmarking
exercises.description: Compare ML-DSA performance with classical signature algorithms.
tasks: Using a cryptography library, measure KeyGen, Sign, Verify times
Compare ML-DSA-65 with Ed25519 and ECDSA P-256
Measure throughput: signatures per second for each algorithm
Analyze the impact of ML-DSA's rejection sampling on sign time variance
Create a performance comparison chart
exercises.starterCode:
# Using Python cryptography library
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ed25519 import Ed25519PrivateKey
import time

def benchmark_signatures(iterations=1000):
    """Benchmark signature operations."""
    
    # Ed25519
    ed_times = {'keygen': [], 'sign': [], 'verify': []}
    
    for _ in range(iterations):
        start = time.perf_counter()
        private_key = Ed25519PrivateKey.generate()
        ed_times['keygen'].append(time.perf_counter() - start)
        
        public_key = private_key.public_key()
        message = b"Test message for benchmarking"
        
        start = time.perf_counter()
        signature = private_key.sign(message)
        ed_times['sign'].append(time.perf_counter() - start)
        
        start = time.perf_counter()
        public_key.verify(signature, message)
        ed_times['verify'].append(time.perf_counter() - start)
    
    # Print results
    import statistics
    for op in ['keygen', 'sign', 'verify']:
        mean_us = statistics.mean(ed_times[op]) * 1e6
        print(f"Ed25519 {op}: {mean_us:.1f} μs")

# Note: For ML-DSA, you would need liboqs or pqcrypto library
benchmark_signatures()
exercises.expectedOutput: Performance comparison showing ML-DSA vs classical algorithm speeds
hints: Install pqcrypto or liboqs-python for ML-DSA benchmarks
ML-DSA signing has high variance due to rejection sampling
Report both mean and standard deviation for signing times
